focus: [D1-2/D1] impeccable per-model <codex>/<gemini> defect blocks vs design-audit ai_fingerprint_tells

graphEvents.label: Impeccable provider-gated detector rules and fixtures
Codex theater/meta-criticism copy tell absent from design-audit catalog

label: Impeccable has provider-specific self-defect blocks and build tests, while design-audit has only a human-readable tell catalog
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE on generated docs and test corpus; ADVISORY for runtime model internalization
recommendation: Add a design-audit tell registry plus generated model self-defect cards and a completeness validator.

label: design-audit is missing impeccable's Codex theater/meta-criticism copy tell
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE for the narrow static text pattern; ADVISORY for broader semantic copy shape
recommendation: Add a Codex tell for theater framing/meta-criticism copy and cross-link it to the interface copy gate.

label: design-audit manual AI-fingerprint testing lacks fixture-backed positive/negative provider-gated expectations
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE on fixture corpus and generated reports; PARTLY ENFORCEABLE on live source; ADVISORY for screenshot-only audits
recommendation: Add clean, Codex, Gemini, and gallery fixtures with expected tell IDs, evidence labels, severity, owner, and score impact.

label: Every model-specific audit tell should map to a registry row, at least one fixture, expected report evidence, severity floor, and owner before it is considered enforceable.
Impeccable filters provider-gated detector findings unless the provider is explicitly enabled, then tests off/on behavior separately.

direction: Add only more prose to ai_fingerprint_tells.md
reason: The prose catalog already exists; the missing enforceable unit is registry-plus-fixture-plus-report evidence.
